records.title: I：P百变莱娜 FAQ 2
records.conclusion:
【②の効果について】
起動効果・誘発効果・誘発即時効果・永続効果のいずれにも分類されない効果です。
リンク召喚されたモンスターは、このカードをリンク素材としてリンク召喚を行ったプレイヤーから見て、相手となるプレイヤーのカードの効果によって破壊されなくなります。
この効果が適用されたリンクモンスターがモンスターゾーンに表側表示で存在しなくなった場合、『相手の効果では破壊されない』効果の適用はなくなります。

records.title: S：P小夜骑士 FAQ 1
records.conclusion:
【①の効果について】
モンスターゾーンで発動できる誘発効果です。
既に自分のモンスターが直接攻撃を行ったターンでも発動できます。（『このターン、自分のモンスターは直接攻撃できない』は効果処理であり、効果処理以降ターン終了時まで適用されます。）
この効果の処理は『そのカードを除外する』で完了です。この効果の処理後、ただちに『このターン、自分のモンスターは直接攻撃できない』効果を適用します。
カードを除外する処理に成功したかに関わらず、この効果が無効にならなかったのであれば、『このターン、自分のモンスターは直接攻撃できない』状態になります。
フィールドに裏側表示で存在するカードを除外する場合、そのカードは表側表示で除外されます。
『このターン、自分のモンスターは直接攻撃できない』効果は、ターン中、自分のモンスターゾーンに存在するモンスターに対して、継続的に適用されます。（モンスターの効果を受けないモンスターは直接攻撃できます。この効果の適用後に自分のモンスターゾーンに存在するようになったモンスターに対しても適用されます。）
steps: 按同步 FAQ 的说明处理。
若对局条件与 FAQ 不同，继续查对应官方 Q&A。

records.title: S：P小夜骑士 FAQ 2
records.conclusion:
【②の効果について】
モンスターゾーンで発動できる誘発即時効果です。
ダメージステップには発動できません。
相手の効果が発動した時に、直接チェーンして発動できます。
処理時に、対象のモンスター２体がモンスターゾーンに存在する場合、『そのモンスター２体をエンドフェイズまで除外する』処理を行います。少なくとも片方が対象から外れた場合、処理は行われません。
処理時に、対象のモンスターが裏側守備表示になっている場合、そのモンスターは裏側表示で除外され、エンドフェイズに裏側守備表示でメインモンスターゾーンに戻ります。
『そのモンスター２体をエンドフェイズまで除外する』処理を行う際に、対象のカードのうち片方がこの効果を受けない場合、もう片方のカードだけがエンドフェイズまで除外されます。
この効果で除外されたモンスターはエンドフェイズに、除外された時の表示形式で、除外された時のコントローラーのメインモンスターゾーンに戻ります。その際にチェーンブロックは作られません。（これは特殊召喚ではありません。）
steps: 按同步 FAQ 的说明处理。
若对局条件与 FAQ 不同，继续查对应官方 Q&A。

records.title: 阿尔白斯之落胤 FAQ 1

【①の効果について】
モンスターゾーンで発動できる誘発効果です。

融合素材とする処理と融合召喚する処理は同時に行われません。
融合素材モンスターは、他の効果が適用されていない限り墓地へ送られますが、他の効果の適用により融合素材が墓地以外のところへ送られることがあります。その場合でも融合素材モンスターがフィールドを離れたのであれば、融合モンスターの特殊召喚は行われます。
処理時に、自分の裏側守備表示のこのカードを融合素材に含めることはできますが、相手の裏側守備表示のモンスターを融合素材に含めることはできません。
steps: 按同步 FAQ 的说明处理。

records.title: I have gained control of a monster by one of the following effects. That monster is then …
records.question:
I have gained control of a monster by one of the following effects. That monster is then banished, and returned to the field, by the effect of <<20245>>. What happens to the monster?

Ⓐ: the effect of <<9861>>.
(i.e., an effect that lets me permanently gain control of the monster)

Ⓑ: the effect of <<4860>>
(i.e., and effect that lets me temporarily gain control of the monster)
records.conclusion:
Ⓐ: You retain control of the monster.
For effects such as that of Big Eye, which do not have a time limit for which you gain control of the monster, control will not return to your opponent even if the card is banished temporarily.

Ⓑ: Control of the monster immediately returns to your opponent.
For effects such as that of <<4860>>, which specify a time limit for which you gain control of the monster, the effect will end if the monster is temporarily banished. Therefore, when the monster returns to the Monster Zone, control immediately returns to the opponent.

records.title: I have Link Summoned <<15739>> by using <<15702>>, <<15975>>, and <<14676>> as the Link M…
records.question:
I have Link Summoned <<15739>> by using <<15702>>, <<15975>>, and <<14676>> as the Link Materials.

Afterwards, the effects of my <<15739>> are negated by my opponent's <<5740>>. What happens to <<14676>>'s『A Link Monster that used this card as material cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects.』effect?
records.conclusion:
That effect still applies.

The『A Link Monster that used this card as material cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects.』effect is applied to any Link Monster that is Link Summoned using <<14676>> as material. (In this case, to <<15739>>.) This is not an effect that is applied by that Link Monster on the field.

Therefore, even if the monster's effects are negated, such as by the effect of <<5740>>, that effect will remain applied to the monster. (The monster will be unable to be destroyed by your opponent's card effects.)

records.title: My opponent resolves the ①st effect of their <<14676>>, performing a Link Summon as a res…
records.question: My opponent resolves the ①st effect of their <<14676>>, performing a Link Summon as a result. Can I now activate the ②nd effect of a <<18178>> in my Graveyard, which can be activated if a monster is Special Summoned by my opponent's activated monster effect?
records.conclusion:
The effect of <<18178>> cannot be activated.

If an effect, like that of <<14676>>, instructs a player to perform a Synchro, Xyz, Pendulum, or Link Summon, the resolution of the Chain Link will grant the player the ability to perform that Summon, but that Summon is then performed after the Chain Link finishes resolving.

records.title: While my opponent's <<23027>> is applying its『if your opponent would Normal or Special Su…
records.question:
While my opponent's <<23027>> is applying its『if your opponent would Normal or Special Summon a monster(s) to their Main Monster Zone, they must use their leftmost usable zone(s)』effect to me, can I Pendulum Summon multiple monsters in each of the following situations?

Ⓐ: <<14676>> is in the left Extra Monster Zone, there are no monsters in the Main Monster Zone, and the only monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my face-up Extra Deck.

Ⓑ: <<14676>> is in the right Extra Monster Zone, there are no monsters in the Main Monster Zone, and the only monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my face-up Extra Deck.

Ⓒ: <<14676>> is in the left Extra Monster Zone, there are no monsters in the Main Monster Zone, and the only monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my face-up Extra Deck and one monster in my hand.

Ⓓ: <<14676>> is in the left Extra Monster Zone, there is one monster in the Main Monster Zone in <<14676>>'s column, and the only monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my face-up Extra Deck

Ⓔ: <<13419>> is in the left Extra Monster Zone, there are no monsters in the Main Monster Zone, and the only monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my face-up Extra Deck

Ⓕ: There are no monsters in the Main Monster Zone, and theo nly monsters I can Pendulum Summon are two monsters in my hand
records.conclusion:
In case Ⓐ, you can only Pendulum Summon one monster. When Pendulum Summoning from the Extra Deck to the Main Monster Zone, you need to Summon to a zone that a Link Monster points to. However, once you place the first monster in the leftmost Main Monster Zone, you would then need to use the Main Monster Zone in the same column as <<14676>> due to <<23027>>'s effect, which no Link Monster points to. Therefore, you cannot Pendulum Summon a second monster.

In case Ⓑ, you cannot Pendulum Summon at all.

In cases Ⓒ, Ⓓ, Ⓔ, and Ⓕ, all listed monsters can be Pendulum Summoned (but you need to use the leftmost usable zone(s)).

records.title: If there are multiple effects to be resolved during the Standby Phase or End Phase, or if…
records.question: If there are multiple effects to be resolved during the Standby Phase or End Phase, or if each player has cards to pay for, or to activate, is there some rule on the order in which all of these happen?
records.conclusion:
At the start of each Phase, the turn player has priority, and can activate/resolve their actions first. If they have multiple actions they can perform, there is no particular order in which they have to activate/resolve them. If the turn player chooses to give up priority, their opponent can also activate/resolve their actions (in no particular order).

If both players have given up priority, if there are any actions that are mandatory to take, the turn player must activate/resolve those actions. If, after all mandatory actions have been dealt with, there are optional actions remaining, these optional actions can still be activated/resolved by the player with priority.

As an example: it is your turn, <<15994>> is in your Graveyard, and your opponent has banished <<19188>> using its own ②nd effect.

Once you reach the End Phase, you, as the turn player, have priority. You must now decide whether to activate <<15994>>'s effect. If you do not wish to activate it, you inform your opponent that you are passing priority. This returns in your opponent gaining priority, and they must decide whether to return <<19188>> to the field. If they choose not to, they can inform you that they are passing priority back to you, and <<19188>> remains banished for now.

At this point, you once again have priority, and must decide whether to activate <<15994>>'s effect. Since <<15994>>'s effect is not mandatory to activate, you can still decide to pass priority back to your opponent at this time. If you do, your opponent gains priority, and will need to return <<19188>> to the field, since doing so is mandatory.

Once they have done so, you regain priority, and can activate <<15994>>'s effect.

records.question:
I have previously resolved the ②nd effect of <<19188>> this turn, banishing one monster from each player's field until the End Phase.

During the End Phase, the effect of my <<5605>> is applying. For each of the following scenarios, can the monsters banished by the effect of <<19188>> be returned to each field?

Ⓐ: There are no monsters on either player's field.
Ⓑ: There are no monsters on my field, but there are monsters on my opponent's field.
Ⓒ: There are monsters on my field, and my opponent controls fewer monsters than me.
Ⓓ: There are monsters on my field, and my opponent controls the same number of monsters as me.
Ⓔ: There are monsters on my field, and my opponent controls more monsters than me.
records.conclusion:
When you return multiple monsters that were temporarily banished by the same effect to the field, you first check if each individual one can be returned to the field. If there are multiple monsters that can be returned, all of them are then returned to the field simultaneously, and if any of them cannot be returned, those monsters are placed in the Graveyard. (This is the case regardless of whose turn it is or who controls <<19188>>.)

To be more specific, the following happens in the respective situations you describe:

ⒶⒷⒸ: When the monsters are returned, there is no restriction on placing monsters on your opponent's field. Therefore, both monsters can be, and are, returned to the field.

ⒹⒺ: When the monsters are returned, you can place monsters on the field, but your opponent cannot. Therefore, only your monster can, and does, return to the field. Your opponent's monster is placed in the Graveyard. (It is not treated as being sent to the Graveyard.)
steps: 按同步 Q&A 的问答结论处理。
若对局条件与问答不同，先回到官方数据库核对完整原文。

records.question:
「<<13549>>」の『①：このターン、モンスター同士の戦闘で発生するお互いの戦闘ダメージは倍になる』効果が適用されているターンです。

自分の永続効果によって『②：このカードが守備表示モンスターを攻撃した場合、その守備力を攻撃力が超えた分の倍の数値だけ戦闘ダメージを与える』状態になっている、攻撃力４０００の「<<12487>>」で、相手フィールドに守備表示で存在する守備力２０００のモンスターを攻撃し、ダメージ計算を行う場合、戦闘ダメージはどうなりますか？
records.conclusion:
結果的に、相手に８０００の戦闘ダメージを与えます。

ダメージ計算に関する複数の効果が適用されている状況では、以下の順に効果を考慮してダメージを決定します。
なお、「<<12487>>」は『倍の数値だけ戦闘ダメージを与える』効果であるため01、「<<13549>>」は『戦闘ダメージは倍になる』効果であるため08に該当します。

01：倍の戦闘ダメージを与える
02：戦闘ダメージはお互いに受ける
03：自分への戦闘ダメージは相手も受ける、戦闘ダメージは代わりに自分／相手が受ける
04：戦闘ダメージは効果ダメージとして扱う
05：戦闘ダメージの数値分だけLPを回復する
06：戦闘ダメージは０になる
07：戦闘ダメージは半分になる
08：戦闘ダメージは倍になる
09：戦闘ダメージは○○○（一定の値）になる
10：○○○以上／以下の戦闘ダメージを受けない
（ただし、06にて戦闘ダメージが０となった場合、それ以降の効果は適用しません。）

質問の状況の場合、自分の「<<12487>>」の攻撃力４０００と、相手モンスターの守備力２０００の差分の倍となる４０００のダメージが、「<<13549>>」の効果によってさらに倍となり、結果的に相手に与えられるダメージは８０００となります。

01に該当する『倍の戦闘ダメージを与える』効果の例
「<<12443>>」①
「<<20217>>」②

08に該当する『戦闘ダメージは倍になる』効果の例
「<<11961>>」③
「<<20506>>」①

records.title: How is damage calculated if <<18017>> equipped with <<14877>> battles in the following si…
records.question:
How is damage calculated if <<18017>> equipped with <<14877>> battles in the following situations?

Ⓐ: When attacking directly.
Ⓑ: When it is in Defense Position and attacked by <<12487>>.
Ⓒ: When it battles <<10777>> and both monsters are in Attack Position.
Ⓓ: When it battles an opponent's <<18017>> and both monsters are in Attack Position.
Ⓔ: When it battles an opponent's <<11213>> and both monsters are in Attack Position.
records.conclusion:
Battle damage is determined by considering different types of effects in the following order. Furthermore, <<12487>> is a "01" effect, <<14877>> is a "02" effect, <<10777>> and <<18017>> are both "03" effects, and <<11213>> is a "08" effect.

01: This card inflicts double battle damage.
02: Battle damage is taken by both players.
03: Battle damage you take is also inflicted to your opponent / your opponent takes any battle damage you would take instead.
04: Battle damage is treated as effect damage.
05: The player gains Life Points instead of taking battle damage.
06: Battle damage becomes 0.
07: Battle damage is halved.
08: Battle damage is doubled.
09: Battle damage becomes X (X being a predetermined value).
10: You do not take battle damage if it is more or less than X.
(However, note that if the Battle Damage has become 0 when applying "06" effects, the remaining effects are not applied.)

(TL note: "01" is <<12487>>, "08" is <<17746>>, for example; these are different kinds of doubling.)

Specifically, the damage in their scenarios is calculated as follows:

Ⓐ: Your opponent takes battle damage equal to the ATK of <<18017>> twice.

Ⓑ: If the DEF of <<18017>> is lower than the ATK of <<12487>>, the controller of <<12487>> takes double battle damage twice. If the DEF of <<18017>> is higher than the ATK of <<12487>>, the controller of <<12487>> takes damage equal to the difference in values twice.

Ⓒ: The controller of <<10777>> takes damage equal to the difference in ATK twice, while the controller of <<18017>> takes damage equal to the difference in ATK once. (If the effect of <<10777>> or <<18017>> make the opponent take battle damage, the effect of the other monster is not applied to that damage.)

Ⓓ: Both players take damage equal to the difference in ATK once. (If the effect of one <<18017>> makes the opponent take battle damage, the effect of the other is not applied to that damage.)

Ⓔ: The controller of <<11213>> takes damage equal to the difference in ATK twice. (Since <<11213>> does not inflict any damage to the opponent, the battle damage is not doubled.)

~~~

Additionally, the following monsters with the『your opponent takes any battle damage you would have taken from battles involving this card instead』effect fall under category "03" as well:
● <<5284>>
● <<12920>>
● <<20773>>
● <<9395>>
● <<10404>>
● <<18016>>
● <<18015>>
● <<18482>>

records.title: There is a monster in my Monster Zone with the effect "your opponent takes any battle dam…
records.question:
There is a monster in my Monster Zone with the effect "your opponent takes any battle damage you would have taken from battles involving this card instead", and it is equipped with <<14877>>.

When that monster battles, if the effect of my opponent's <<11799>> is applied that reduces the next battle damage they take to 0, what happens?
records.conclusion:
Battle damage is determined by considering different types of effects in the following order.

01: Inflicts double battle damage.
02: Battle damage is taken by both players.
03: Battle damage you take is also inflicted to your opponent / your opponent takes any battle damage you would take instead.
04: Battle damage is treated as effect damage.
05: The player gains Life Points instead of taking battle damage.
06: Battle damage becomes 0.
07: Battle damage is halved.
08: Battle damage is doubled.
09: Battle damage becomes X (X being a predetermined value).
10: You do not take battle damage if it is more or less than X.
(However, note that if the Battle Damage has become 0 when applying "06" effects, the remaining effects are not applied.)

Furthermore, <<14877>> is a "02" effect, the monster with the "your opponent takes any battle damage you would have taken from battles involving this card instead" effect is a "03" effect, and <<11799>> is a "06" effect.

In this scenario, first consider the effect of <<14877>>, then consider the effect of your monster with the "your opponent takes any battle damage you would have taken from battles involving this card instead" effect for the damage you would take. As a result, your opponent would take battle damage twice during 1 damage calculation (the overlap of a "02" effect and "03" effect can cause a player to take battle damage twice). However, since the two instances of battle damage occur at the same time, the final outcome is that your opponent takes 0 damage due to the effect of <<11799>>.
steps: 按同步 Q&A 的问答结论处理。
若对局条件与问答不同，先回到官方数据库核对完整原文。

records.title: (A) If the ①st effect of <<17600>> resolves, equipping both <<5389>> and <<5728>> to itse…
records.question:
(A) If the ①st effect of <<17600>> resolves, equipping both <<5389>> and <<5728>> to itself, will <<5389>> be destroyed?

(B) If the ③rd effect of <<18017>> equips <<5389>> to a monster that is already equipped with <<5728>>, will <<5389>> be destroyed?

(C) If a monster is already equipped with <<5728>>, and <<5389>> is activated by targeting it, will <<5389>> be destroyed?

Additionally, in all of these scenarios, what happens to <<5389>>'s『When this card is destroyed by a card effect while equipped to a monster: Look at your opponent's hand and discard 1 card from their hand.』effect?
records.conclusion:
In scenarios (A) and (B), <<5389>> is equipped while resolving a card effect. As soon as this has been done (thus targeting the equipped monster), that equipped <<5389>> will be destroyed by the effect of <<5728>>. Since <<5389>> is destroyed while equipped, its effect will then activate. (Since the『Look at your opponent's hand and discard 1 card from their hand.』effect does not target the monster equipped with <<5728>>, it will not be negated by <<5728>>'s effect, and will be applied normally.)

This also applies to an Equip Card being equipped by the following cards' effects: <<15276>>, <<6456>>, <<18228>>, <<13430>>, <<18026>>, and <<14886>>.

If you activate the Equip Spell Card <<5389>>, you activate it by targeting a monster, and it would then be equipped to the targeted monster when its activation resolves. However, if the targeted monster is equipped with <<5728>> at this time, the effect of <<5389>>'s activation will be negated, and <<5389>> will be destroyed before it can be equipped.
Since <<5389>> is destroyed without being equipped to the targeted monster, its『When this card is destroyed by a card effect while equipped to a monster:』 effect cannot not be activated afterwards.

I activate <<7445>>, or activate <<15245>>' effect, by discarding <<16194>> as a cost.

After this activation resolves, can the effect of <<16194>>, which activates if it is sent to the Graveyard or banished by a card effect, be activated?
records.conclusion:
In this case, <<16194>> was discarded as a cost to activate an effect. It was not sent to the Graveyard by a card effect. Thus, the effect of <<16194>> cannot be activated.

The effect of <<16194>> is an effect that can be activated when <<16194>> is sent to the Graveyard by a card effect, or is banished.

However, if the situation above occurs while the effect of <<6682>> is applying, the <<16194>> will not be discarded to the Graveyard. Instead, it will be banished by the effect of <<6682>>. As <<16194>> was banished by a card effect, the effect of <<16194>> can then be activated.

My opponent resolves the effect of <<15245>> (or <<12834>>), sends a <<18178>> in my Monster Zone to the Graveyard as Fusion Material, and performs a Fusion Summon.

My opponent has thus Special Summoned a Fusion Monster from their Extra Deck. Can I now activate the ②nd effect of that same <<18178>> that was sent to the Graveyard as Fusion Material?
records.conclusion:
Yes, you can.

In order for the ②nd effect of <<18178>> to be activated, that same <<18178>> must already be in the Graveyard at the time that a monster is Special Summoned by your opponent's activated monster effect.

In the situation you describe, a Fusion Monster is Fusion Summoned to your opponent's field, using <<18178>> as material. When you perform a Fusion Summon, Fusion Materials are treated as having been sent to the Graveyard strictly before the Fusion Monster is Special Summoned. Therefore, the activation condition for <<18178>>'s ②nd effect has been met.

records.title: Ⓐ: My opponent only controls a single monster, and I declare an attack on that monster us…
records.question:
Ⓐ: My opponent only controls a single monster, and I declare an attack on that monster using my <<19360>>. That monster leaves the field due to a card effect, and a replay occurs. Can <<19360>> attack directly?

Ⓑ: My <<19360>> attacks my opponent directly, and damage calculation is performed. Afterwards, a card effect Special Summons a monster to my opponent's field. Can <<19360>> attack that monster?

Ⓒ: My <<19360>> attacks my opponent's only monster, and that monster is destroyed by the battle. Can <<19360>> then attack directly?

Ⓓ: My opponent controls two monsters, and my <<19360>>, equipped with <<5725>>, attacks directly. During the Battle Step, <<5725>> is destroyed by a card effect.
That causes a replay, in the course of which <<19360>> attacks and destroy one of my opponent's monsters. Can it now attack my opponent's other monster, as well?
records.conclusion:
Ⓐ: Yes, you can attack directly.
In addition, if <<19360>> attacked another monster this turn, it cannot attack directly, so the attack ends.

Ⓑ: Since damage calculation has been performed for the direct attack, you cannot attack the other monster.

Ⓒ: You cannot attack directly.

Ⓓ: You cannot attack the other monster.
Since <<19360>> has declared a direct attack, it cannot『attack all your opponent's monsters, once each』due to its effect, even if the direct attack's target changes to a monster during a replay.

「<<17355>>」の『①：お互いのメインフェイズに、相手のメインモンスターゾーンの効果モンスター１体を対象として発動できる。その相手モンスターをそれと同じ縦列の相手の魔法＆罠ゾーンに永続魔法カード扱いとして表側表示で置く（置く先にあるカードは破壊され、それがモンスターカードの場合、相手はその攻撃力分のLPを失う）』のモンスター効果を適用したことによって、装備魔法カードや永続魔法カードとして同じ縦列の魔法＆罠ゾーンに存在する以下のモンスタートークンが破壊された場合、攻撃力分のライフポイントは失いますか？

（A）「<<14741>>」の効果で特殊召喚されたのちに魔法＆罠ゾーンに置かれた「原始生命態トークン」

（B）「<<16748>>」の効果で特殊召喚されたのちに魔法＆罠ゾーンに置かれた「勇者トークン」
records.conclusion:
そのモンスタートークンが元々の攻撃力を持つモンスタートークンである場合には、破壊されたモンスタートークンの攻撃力分のライフポイントを失います。ここでのモンスタートークンの元々の攻撃力とは、そのモンスタートークンを特殊召喚したカードに記載されている攻撃力を指します。

（A）の場合、「<<14741>>」のテキストに記載されている「原始生命態トークン」の攻撃力は？ですので、ライフポイントは失いません。

（B）の場合、「<<16748>>」のテキストに記載されている「勇者トークン」の攻撃力は２０００ですので、ライフポイントを２０００失います。
steps: 按同步 Q&A 的问答结论处理。
若对局条件与问答不同，先回到官方数据库核对完整原文。

records.title: The activation of a monster's effect is negated due to the『Once per turn, when another mo…
records.question:
The activation of a monster's effect is negated due to the『Once per turn, when another monster's effect is activated (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation, and if you do, destroy that monster, and if you do that, this card gains ATK equal to the destroyed monster's original ATK until the end of this turn』effect of <<12321>>, and its ATK is increased.

In this situation, if <<13631>> is activated by targeting <<12321>>, and the effects of <<12321>> are negated, what happens to the increased ATK?
records.conclusion:
The ATK increasing effect will no longer be applied.

From among monster effects that change ATK/DEF, the ones that can only ever be applied to the monster itself will no longer apply when the effects of that monster are negated. records.question:
While there are no monsters in my Monster Zone, I attempt to Special Summon a monster. During this attempt, my opponent activates <<12627>>'s『negate the Special Summon』effect.

Can I activate <<12070>>'s『when your opponent activates a monster effect while you control no monsters』effect in response?
records.conclusion:
発動できます。

自分のモンスターはまだ特殊召喚に成功していませんので、フィールドに存在する扱いにはなっていません。
したがって、「<<12070>>」の効果の発動条件である『自分フィールドにモンスターが存在せず、相手モンスターの効果が発動した時』を満たしています。

自分フィールドに他のカードが存在しない状況で、自分のモンスターの特殊召喚を無効にする効果を相手が発動した時、以下の効果の発動条件も満たされます。

『自分・相手ターンに、自分フィールドにカードが存在しない場合、このカードを手札から捨てて発動できる』

「<<20500>>」
「<<20206>>」

records.title: My opponent controls >, and my > battles my opponent's Toon monster. As a result, it is d…
records.question:
My opponent controls >, and my > battles my opponent's Toon monster. As a result, it is determined that that Toon monster will be destroyed by the battle.

After damage calculation, I now activate >'s『send 1 "Sky Striker" card from your Deck to the Graveyard』effect.

When this effect resolves, can my opponent apply >'s『before resolving another activated card or effect, you can banish 1 Toon monster you control until immediately after that card/effect resolves』effect to banish that same Toon monster that was determined to be destroyed by the battle?
cards: 完全なる世界 トゥーン・ワールド
完美世界 卡通世界
records.conclusion:
Yes, they can.

If they do, and that monster is banished and then returns to the Monster Zone, it will no longer be considered to be determined to be destroyed by battle, and will remain in the Monster Zone.

records.title: （A）「<<14328>>」の『①：相手フィールドの表側表示モンスター１体を対象としてこのカードを発動できる。そのモンスターのコントロールを得る。そのモンスターは自分フィールドに…
records.question:
（A）「<<14328>>」の『①：相手フィールドの表側表示モンスター１体を対象としてこのカードを発動できる。そのモンスターのコントロールを得る。そのモンスターは自分フィールドに存在する場合、攻撃できず、効果を発動できない。そのモンスターがフィールドから離れた時にこのカードは破壊される』効果を発動し、その同一チェーン上で発動した効果によって、対象のモンスターが裏側守備表示になった場合、処理はどうなりますか？

（B）「<<14328>>」を発動したことによって、相手モンスターのコントロールを得ています。
この状態で、他の効果が適用されたことによって対象のモンスターが裏側守備表示になった場合、適用されている「<<14328>>」の効果はどうなりますか？
records.conclusion:
If the monster is flipped face-down after <<14328>> has resolved, <<14328>> stops targeting that monster.

Therefore,『take control of that monster』stops applying, and control of the monster returns to your opponent. Additionally, since『while you control that monster, it cannot attack or activate effects』stops applying, the monster will be able to attack, and its effects can be activated.

<<14328>> will remain face-up in the Spell & Trap Zone without a target, and thus『when that monster leaves the field, destroy this card』will also no longer apply.
steps: 按同步 Q&A 的问答结论处理。
若对局条件与问答不同，先回到官方数据库核对完整原文。

records.title: 「<<22960>>」の『①：手札のこのカードを相手に見せ、フィールドのモンスター１体を対象として発動できる。そのモンスターを破壊し、手札から雷族モンスター１体を特殊召喚する。こ…
records.question:
「<<22960>>」の『①：手札のこのカードを相手に見せ、フィールドのモンスター１体を対象として発動できる。そのモンスターを破壊し、手札から雷族モンスター１体を特殊召喚する。このターン、自分は手札からしか効果モンスターを特殊召喚できない』の効果の対象に「<<23173>>」を選択しました。

その効果処理時に「<<23173>>」の『②：フィールドのこのカードが戦闘・効果で破壊される場合、代わりに自分か相手のフィールドの表側表示カード１枚を破壊できる』効果を適用して対象の「<<23173>>」以外を破壊した場合、『手札から雷族モンスター１体を特殊召喚する』効果は適用されますか？

また、『このターン、自分は手札からしか効果モンスターを特殊召喚できない』効果はどうなりますか。
records.conclusion:
「<<22960>>」の①の効果の処理時に「<<23173>>」の②の効果を適用して対象の「<<23173>>」以外を破壊した場合、「<<22960>>」の効果で対象のカードを破壊できていないため、『手札から雷族モンスター１体を特殊召喚する』効果は適用されません。

なお、その場合でも、『このターン、自分は手札からしか効果モンスターを特殊召喚できない』状態になります。

records.title: I control <<13763>>, which has the『This card can attack directly』effect. My opponent cont…
records.question:
I control <<13763>>, which has the『This card can attack directly』effect. My opponent controls either <<14297>> (with the『their monsters cannot target monsters for attacks, except this one』effect) or <<18733>> (with the『your opponent's monsters can only target monsters you control with the highest ATK for attacks』effect).

In this situation, can <<13763>> attack directly?
records.conclusion:
Even if the effect of your opponent's <<14297>> is applying, you can attack directly with <<13763>>. The『their monsters cannot target monsters for attacks, except this one』effect only prevents you from targeting their other monsters with attacks. It does not prevent you from targeting the opposing player.

If the effect of your opponent's <<18733>> is applying, you cannot attack directly with <<13763>>. The『your opponent's monsters can only target monsters you control with the highest ATK for attacks』effect restricts choosing attack targets in general. Therefore, it also prevents you from attacking directly.

~~~

The following are other examples of cards whose effects restrict the choice of attack target:
● <<15702>>

● <<13789>>

records.title: If a Ritual Monster is destroyed by battle while <<22121>>, <<22120>>, and <<22122>> are …
records.question: If a Ritual Monster is destroyed by battle while <<22121>>, <<22120>>, and <<22122>> are in the Graveyard, can the『When your Ritual Monster is destroyed by battle while this card is in your Graveyard: You can Special Summon this card』effect of each one be activated in the same Chain?
records.conclusion:
They can be activated.

Only 1 Trigger Effect that Special Summons the card itself, and that can be activated in the hand, can be activated in the same Chain, but the effects of <<22121>>, <<22120>>, and <<22122>> are Trigger Effects that can only be activated in the Graveyard, so since they aren't effects that can be activated in the hand, they're not considered to be "Trigger Effects that Special Summon themselves and that can be activated in the hand".

records.title: While the『Your Toon monsters can attack directly』effect of my <<23160>> is being applied,…
records.question:
While the『Your Toon monsters can attack directly』effect of my <<23160>> is being applied, can my Toon monsters in the following situations attack directly?

Ⓐ: My <<4773>> which says『Can attack your opponent directly, unless they control a Toon monster, in which case this card must target a Toon monster for its attacks』, if there's a Toon monster face-up in the opponent's Monster Zone

Ⓑ: My <<12431>> which says『While you control "<<4798>>" and your opponent controls no Toon monsters, this card can attack your opponent directly』, if there's a <<4798>> on my field, and there's a Toon monster and a non-Toon monster in the opponent's Monster Zone
records.conclusion:
Ⓐ: It cannot attack directly.
This is because even though the effect of your <<23160>> is being applied, your <<4773>> says『this card must target a Toon monster for its attacks』. (It will attack a Toon monster that's face-up in the opponent's Monster Zone.)

Ⓑ: It can attack directly.
The text of <<12431>> doesn't say『this card must target a Toon monster for its attacks』, so it can, attack the opponent's Toon monster, attack their non-Toon monster, or attack directly. (It cannot attack directly if the effect of your <<23160>> isn't being applied and there's a face-up Toon monster in the opponent's Monster Zone.)

~~~

Like Ⓐ, this is the same for the following cards that say『Can attack your opponent directly, unless they control a Toon monster, in which case this card must target a Toon monster for its attacks』:

● <<4734>>

● <<5450>>

records.title: I resolve the『target 1 other face-up card you control; destroy it, then add 1 face-up Pen…
records.question:
I resolve the『target 1 other face-up card you control; destroy it, then add 1 face-up Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck to your hand』effect of <<13507>>, destroying an <<12906>> in my Pendulum Zone before adding it to my hand.

After this resolves, can I activate that <<12906>>'s『if a card(s) you control is destroyed by battle or card effect』effect?
records.conclusion:
Yes, you can.

Since <<12906>> is added to your hand while resolving <<13507>>'s effect, it will be in your hand by the time it is time to activate its『if a card(s) you control is destroyed by battle or card effect』effect.

However, note that that <<12906>> is not a『monster (...) that was destroyed this turn,』and therefore you cannot choose it as the monster to『add 1 monster with the same name from your Deck to your hand.』

Additionally, if, after resolving <<13507>>'s effect, <<13507>>'s『draw 1 card』effect activates, note that this effect forms Chain Link 1. Therefore, your opponent has the opportunity to respond before you can activate <<12906>>'s effect, which you can then activate as Chain Link 2 or higher.
(However, if your hand is revealed by an effect such as <<23162>>, you can activate <<12906>>'s effect as Chain Link 2 immediately, without your opponent having the opportunity to respond.)

(TL note: allowing the opponent to respond before activating Astrograph's effect is OCG specific, due to how Trigger effects are handled differently there.)

Similarly, if you use that effect of <<13507>> to destroy an <<22704>> and add it to your hand, you can then activate <<22704>>'s『if a face-up Monster Card(s) you control is destroyed by battle or card effect』effect.

records.title: My opponent controls a <<16300>>, which is "unaffected by your opponent's (= my) activate…
records.question:
My opponent controls a <<16300>>, which is "unaffected by your opponent's (= my) activated effects".

What happens if the effects of <<5740>>, <<14328>>, <<12668>>, <<4898>>, or <<17069>>'s ③rd effect, are applied in this situation?
records.conclusion:
The effects of <<5740>> and <<12668>> will negate the effects of <<16300>>, and the effect of <<14328>> and <<4898>> will take control of <<16300>>. <<17069>>'s effect will destroy <<16300>> during the End Phase.

Monsters which are『unaffected by activated effects』will be unaffected by any effect that is applied when the Chain Link formed by an activation resolves. However, while the effects of <<5740>>, <<12668>>, <<14328>>, <<4898>>, <<12205>>, <<23162>>, etc. are effects that begin applying when the Chain Link formed by the card activation resolves, these effects will continue to apply to monsters afterwards. Therefore, these effects can affect monsters that are "unaffected by activated effects".

Similarly, <<17069>>'s『destroy all monsters your opponent controls during the End Phase of this turn』effect also applies after the Chain Link formed by its activation has finished resolving. Therefore, it can also affect monsters that are "unaffected by activated effects".

records.title: Can the effect of <<13587>> be chained to the『●Special Summon 1 "<<4007>>" from your hand…
records.question: Can the effect of <<13587>> be chained to the『●Special Summon 1 "<<4007>>" from your hand or Graveyard』effect of <<16653>> in a situation where there's no <<4007>> in the Graveyard?
records.conclusion:
Yes, you can.
(The first『●』effect of <<16653>> is an effect that『includes an effect that Special Summons a monster from the Graveyard,』regardless of whether there is a <<4007>> in the Graveyard when it is activated.)

In addition, if the effect of <<13587>> is chained when the first『●』effect is used at the same time as the card activation of <<16653>>, the card activation of <<16653>> will be negated, so it will be sent to the Graveyard.
In this case, <<16653>> isn't treated to be sent from the Spell & Trap Zone to the Graveyard, so the『If this face-up card is sent from the Spell & Trap Zone to the Graveyard: Destroy all monsters you control』effect won't be activated.

(If the effect of <<13587>> is chained when the first『●』effect is activated while <<16653>> is already face-up in the Spell & Trap Zone, the activation of the『●』effect will be negated, but <<16653>> will remain in the Spell & Trap Zone.)

records.title: I have activated <<16653>> while I control a <<16809>>, whose『other cards you control can…
records.question:
I have activated <<16653>> while I control a <<16809>>, whose『other cards you control cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects』effect is applying.

The『negate the activation, and if you do, destroy that card』effect of my opponent's <<16386>> now resolves in response. Will <<16653>> be destroyed?
records.conclusion:
The activation of <<16653>> will be negated, and it will be destroyed.

Even though cards on your field cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects, if a card's activation is negated and the card is then destroyed, this is not considered to be destroying a card on your field.

This also applies if the activation of a Spell/Trap Card is negated, and it is destroyed, while any of the following effects are applying:
● <<13357>>'s effect

records.title: The ②nd effect of <<18739>> Special Summons a『Level 2 or 3 "Nouvelles" Ritual Monster fro…
records.question: The ②nd effect of <<18739>> Special Summons a『Level 2 or 3 "Nouvelles" Ritual Monster from your hand or Deck』. However, this effect does not perform a Ritual Summon. Why is it possible to Special Summon a "Nouvelles" Ritual Monster this way?
records.conclusion:
Unless there is text on a Ritual Monster that limits the ways in which it can be Special Summoned, a Ritual Monster can also be Special Summoned from the hand or Deck by an effect that specifically allows you to Special Summon a Ritual Monster.

Additionally, as a general rule, Ritual Monsters in your hand or Deck can only be Special Summoned by Ritual Summoning them, however, as an exception, they can be Special Summoned by effects that ignore Summoning conditions.

"Effects that specifically allow you to Special Summon a Ritual Monster" include the following effects. However, even if the Ritual Monster is Special Summoned by one of the above exceptional methods, this will not be treated as properly Special Summoning the Ritual Monster. If the monster Special Summoned this way is then sent to the Graveyard, or banished, it can no longer be Special Summoned, except by Ritual Summon.

~~~

records.title: If <<11116>> is activated in a Chain that contains monster effects activated outside of t…
records.question: If <<11116>> is activated in a Chain that contains monster effects activated outside of the field, or the activated effects of Spell/Trap Cards, what happens?
records.conclusion:
If <<11116>> is activated, and the Chain contains monster effects that activated outside of the field (such as effects activated from the hand or Graveyard), those activations are also negated. However, those monsters do not return to the Deck. (Only cards on the field that had their activation negated will be shuffled into the Deck.)

In addition, if the Chain that <<11116>> was activated in also contains the activated effect of a Spell/Trap Card in the Graveyard, or the activation effect of a Spell/Trap Card already face-up on the field, that effect will not be negated.

Note that, even if the effect activated immediately before <<11116>>'s effect is such a Spell/Trap effect, <<11116>> can still be activated, as long as a monster effect, or a Spell/Trap Card, was activated at any previous point during the Chain. (This also applies to <<6917>>.)
steps: 按同步 Q&A 的问答结论处理。

records.question:
『①：S召喚したこのカードがフィールドに表側表示で存在する限り１度だけ、このカードより攻撃力が高いモンスターがフィールドに存在する場合に発動できる。フィールドの攻撃力が一番高いモンスター１体を破壊する。この効果は相手ターンでも発動できる』効果を既に発動している「<<12065>>」が自分のモンスターゾーンに表側表示で存在しています。

その後に、その「<<12065>>」が、他の効果によってコントロールが相手のモンスターゾーンに移った場合、相手はその「<<12065>>」の効果を発動できますか？
records.conclusion:
No, they cannot.

The effect of <<12065>> can only be used once while it remains face-up on the field. Therefore, even if control of <<12065>> changes to your opponent after you have used its effect, your opponent cannot use that <<12065>>'s effect.

~~~

records.conclusion:
【③の効果について】
フィールドゾーンで適用できるチェーンブロックの作られない効果です。
このカード以外のカードの効果が発動した効果のチェーンブロックの処理時に（処理の開始時点で）自分のモンスターゾーンに表側表示で存在するトゥーンモンスターを、その効果処理後まで除外できます。
ダメージステップ中でも適用できます。
このカードを発動したチェーンブロックの処理が完了した後から適用できます。（このカードの発動にチェーンして発動した効果が適用される際には適用できません。）
この効果を適用したチェーンブロックの効果処理後に使用可能な自分のメインモンスターゾーンがない場合、この効果で除外したモンスターは墓地に置かれます。（効果で墓地へ送られた扱いにはなりません。）
この効果適用後、その発動した効果でこのカードがフィールドゾーンに存在しなくなった場合や、このカードの効果が無効になっている場合でも、除外したモンスターはフィールドに戻ります。
steps: 按命中的卡片 FAQ 处理。
